data.title: Aave Initiates Recovery Plan for rsETH Bridge Exploit
data.description: Aave has announced a recovery plan to address the rsETH bridge exploit, with the potential to recover approximately 13,000 ETH. According to NS3.AI, the initiative seeks to restore the leaked rsETH collateral to a value of 1.017 ETH. The recovery process begins with a governance proposal and includes a temporary adjustment of the rsETH price. During this period, related assets will remain frozen until the recovery is finalized.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump's Approval Rating Declines to 34%
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's approval rating has decreased to 34%, according to a recent Reuters/Ipsos poll. This marks a two-point drop from the 36% approval rating recorded in mid-April. According to NS3.AI, the decline in approval ratings may reflect growing public dissatisfaction with the current administration's policies and actions. The poll results highlight the challenges faced by Trump as he navigates various domestic and international issues. Analysts suggest that the president's handling of key matters could be influencing public perception and contributing to the downward trend in approval ratings.

data.title: Ethereum Token uPEG Reaches Record Market Cap
data.description:
Ethereum ecosystem token uPEG briefly reached a market capitalization of $12 million, marking a historic high, according to ChainCatcher. The current market cap stands at $11.22 million, with a 24-hour increase of 52.7% and a trading volume of $3.7 million.

Unipeg, the token behind uPEG, focuses on creating on-chain objects using Uniswap V4 Hooks. Each transaction automatically generates a unique 24×24 SVG pixel art unicorn image, driven entirely by on-chain mechanisms without the need for artists or minting.

Previously, on April 25, OpenSea CMO Adam Hollander purchased a small amount of uPEG, expressing interest in experimenting with the concept. This action led to a temporary tripling of uPEG's value.

ChainCatcher advises users to exercise caution due to the significant price volatility of related tokens.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Amazon AWS and OpenAI Expand Partnership
data.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) and OpenAI have announced an expansion of their partnership. According to Jin10, the collaboration aims to enhance the integration of AI technologies into AWS's cloud services, providing customers with advanced AI capabilities. This move is expected to leverage OpenAI's expertise in artificial intelligence to improve AWS's offerings and support the growing demand for AI-driven solutions.

data.title: Iran Asserts Right to Counter Security Threats in Territorial Waters
data.description: Iran's Permanent Mission to the United Nations issued a statement on the 28th via social media, asserting Iran's right to take all necessary measures within its territorial waters to address security threats. According to Odaily, the statement emphasized Iran's legal right, as a major coastal state of the Strait of Hormuz, to implement necessary and proportional actions against emerging security threats. Iran highlighted that these measures aim to ensure safe navigation and prevent the Strait of Hormuz from being used for hostile or military purposes. The statement accused the United States of illegal actions that endanger international navigation safety and reiterated that any disruption of transportation in the Persian Gulf region and its consequences should be the responsibility of the U.S.

data.title: Judge Urges Tech Executives to Limit Social Media Use Amid Musk-OpenAI Lawsuit
data.description: On April 29, Jin10 reported that the judge overseeing the lawsuit between Elon Musk and OpenAI has urged tech executives involved in the case to limit their social media activity. This request follows a series of social media attacks by Musk, the world's richest person, against OpenAI's leadership, calling its CEO a 'fraud Sam Altman.' Ahead of Tuesday's court proceedings, U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers asked Musk and his peers at OpenAI to 'control their use of social media to prevent exacerbating matters outside the courtroom.' Musk, Altman, and OpenAI President Greg Brockman have all agreed to comply with the judge's request.

data.title: Iran reiterated its right to take measures to safeguard the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: The Iranian Permanent Mission to the United Nations issued a statement on social media on the 28th, stating that Iran has the right to take all necessary measures to address security threats within its territorial waters. The statement pointed out that as the main coastal state in the territorial waters containing the Strait of Hormuz, Iran has the legitimate right to take necessary and reciprocal measures to address emerging security threats. Iran emphasized that these measures are aimed at ensuring safe navigation and preventing the Strait of Hormuz from being used for hostile or military purposes. The statement accused the United States of illegally endangering international navigation safety. The statement also reiterated that the United States should bear responsibility for any disruption to shipping in the Persian Gulf region and its consequences. (CCTV News)

data.title: AI Stocks to Watch: AMD, Palantir, and Super Micro Computer Ahead of Earnings
data.description: The analysis identifies three AI stocks to monitor as they approach their earnings reports on May 4–5: AMD, Palantir, and Super Micro Computer. According to NS3.AI, AMD is maintaining a bull flag pattern close to its $314.69 support level. Meanwhile, Palantir is revisiting a descending channel breakout near $140.78. Super Micro Computer continues its relief rally following a sell-off in March.

data.title: The cryptocurrency market saw $195 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, including $129 million in long positions.
data.description: According to CoinGlass monitoring, 67,425 people worldwide were liquidated in the past 24 hours, with a total liquidation amount of $195 million. Of this, $129 million were long positions liquidated and $65.743 million were short positions liquidated. In the past 12 hours, the total liquidation amount was $149 million, with $109 million in long positions liquidated and $39.9961 million in short positions liquidated. In the past 4 hours, the total liquidation amount was $62.4241 million, with $52.7277 million in long positions liquidated and $9.6964 million in short positions liquidated. In the past hour, the total liquidation amount was $2.997 million, with $915,300 in long positions liquidated and $2.0817 million in short positions liquidated. The largest single liquidation occurred on the Hyperliquid platform's XYZ:CL-USD trading pair, worth $6,512,600.

data.title: World Network's WLD Token Plummets Amid Legal Proceedings
data.description: World Network's WLD token has experienced a significant decline, dropping 98% from its all-time high. According to NS3.AI, the token fell nearly 3% as jury selection commenced on Monday in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI leadership. The network's daily transactions have decreased from 4.7 million in September last year to 1.26 million yesterday. Additionally, World Network's daily trading volume has plummeted 99%, from 17.67 million in September to 151,000 on Monday.

data.title: NVIDIA Launches Open-Source Multimodal Model Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
data.description: NVIDIA announced the release of its open-source multimodal model, Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, on the X platform today. According to Odaily, the model features a 30B-A3B mixture of experts architecture (MoE) and supports a 256K context, enabling unified processing of video, audio, image, and text inputs. Compared to similar open-source models with interactive capabilities, Nemotron 3 Nano Omni offers a ninefold increase in throughput, significantly reducing inference costs and enhancing scalability. The model is now available on Hugging Face, OpenRouter, and NVIDIA NIM, and has been adopted by companies such as Aible, Applied Scientific Intelligence, and H Company.

data.title: Experts Highlight Challenges and Opportunities in Financial Systems Amid AI and Crypto Advancements
data.description: Microsoft's Bill Borden has highlighted the increasing pressure on legacy financial systems due to the rising demands of AI-driven transactions. According to NS3.AI, Borden emphasized the need for enhanced audit and control tools to manage these demands effectively. Meanwhile, Chainalysis CEO Jonathan Levin pointed out that the existing crypto infrastructure provides a viable model for software-driven finance. Levin predicted that within the next decade, a significant portion of commerce could be settled on public infrastructure, reflecting a shift towards more decentralized financial systems.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Oracle Expresses Excitement Over Collaboration with OpenAI
data.description: Oracle has expressed its enthusiasm regarding its collaboration with OpenAI. According to Jin10, the partnership is expected to enhance Oracle's capabilities in artificial intelligence and machine learning. The collaboration aims to leverage OpenAI's advanced technologies to improve Oracle's product offerings and services. This move is seen as a strategic effort by Oracle to strengthen its position in the rapidly evolving AI industry.

data.title: Jack Dorsey and Ben McKenzie Offer Divergent Views on Bitcoin
data.description: Jack Dorsey has expressed his belief that Bitcoin serves as a form of money. According to NS3.AI, this perspective contrasts sharply with actor Ben McKenzie's view, who has labeled cryptocurrencies as a Ponzi scheme. Additionally, the article notes that both the SEC and the CFTC categorize Bitcoin as a non-security digital asset, aligning it with commodities.

data.title: Coinbase will launch VIRTUAL spot trading.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Coinbase will launch spot trading of Virtuals Protocol (VIRTUAL) on April 29. If liquidity conditions are met and trading is supported, the VIRTUAL trading pair will open at or after 9:00 AM Pacific Time.

data.title: Over Protocol announced it is ceasing operations and shutting down its infrastructure.
data.description: The Over Foundation announced on the X platform that it is ceasing operations of Over Protocol due to financial constraints. All infrastructure and services, including OverWallet, OverNode, OverFlex, RPC endpoints, block explorers, and APIs, have been shut down, with no plans to resume operations. While Over Protocol was designed as a decentralized Layer 1 mainnet, with the foundation ceasing services, the network is highly likely to become unusable in practice. Continued block production will depend on independent validators running open-source client software.

data.title: NVIDIA introduces the Nemotron 3 Nano Omni model, offering a 9x improvement in multimodal inference efficiency.
data.description: NVIDIA announced the release of the open-source multimodal model Nemotron 3 Nano Omni on its X platform. This model employs a 30B-A3B hybrid expert architecture (MoE), supports 256K context, and can uniformly handle video, audio, image, and text input. Compared to similarly interactive open-source all-around models, this model offers up to 9x higher throughput, significantly reduces inference costs, and improves scalability. Nemotron 3 Nano Omni is currently available on Hugging Face, OpenRouter, and NVIDIA NIM, and has been adopted by companies such as Aible, Applied Scientific Intelligence, and H Company.

data.title: Over Foundation Halts Operations Due to Funding Constraints
data.description: The Over Foundation has announced the cessation of operations for its Over Protocol infrastructure and services due to financial limitations. According to Foresight News, the affected services include OverWallet, OverNode, OverFlex, RPC nodes, block explorers, and API interfaces. The foundation has also stated that there are no plans to resume these services.

data.title: AI Recruitment Startup Dex Secures $5.3 Million in Seed Funding
data.description:
AI recruitment startup Dex has announced the completion of a $5.3 million seed funding round, led by Notion Capital. According to ChainCatcher, other participants included a16z Speedrun, Concept Ventures, and angel investors from OpenAI and other companies, bringing Dex's total funding to $8.4 million.

Founded by former Atomico advisor Paddy Lambros, Dex positions itself as an 'AI talent agency,' focusing on high-end technical talent such as AI researchers and software engineers. The platform currently has over 15,000 registered engineers and serves more than 50 tech companies, including ElevenLabs and Synthesia. Dex interacts with job seekers through voice or text, using models from OpenAI and Anthropic to build candidate profiles, which are then matched directly with employers.

Unlike traditional software sales, Dex charges a fee of 20%-30% of the candidate's salary upon successful recruitment. The company reported an annualized revenue of approximately $1.8 million since it began charging fees at the end of 2025, with expectations to achieve profitability within the year.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Warns of Potential Credit Market Downturn
data.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has issued a warning that the credit market downturn could be more severe than anticipated, despite strong quarterly results from his bank and Wall Street competitors. According to Jin10, Dimon spoke at a meeting hosted by Norway's central bank investment management company on Tuesday, highlighting concerns in the private credit sector, which includes over 1,000 companies. He suggested that while some companies may perform exceptionally well, not all will withstand a cyclical reversal. Dimon emphasized that underwriting standards have not faced a credit recession in a long time, and when the next one occurs, it could be worse than expected. He added that this situation might also affect some banks adversely.
